Profile01

Who I Am & Why It Matters

A professional whose mathematical foundation gives every analytical output a layer of precision most cannot offer.

"I believe that data without context is noise — the true skill of an analyst lies in translating numbers into decisions that move organisations forward."

BSc Mathematics from MGKVP Varanasi provides the quantitative foundation. 12 Google certifications — including the full Google Advanced Data Analytics Professional Certificate — keep me at the cutting edge of applied AI and data science.

Experience03

Professional Experience

Data & Operations Analyst
Family Business · Varanasi, India · Ongoing
⬤ Business Operations & Analytics
Managed complete business data operations — compiling, cleaning, and structuring raw data into professional decision-ready reports for leadership.
Designed and maintained Excel-based financial record systems tracking monthly expenditure across multiple categories with full variance analysis.

Overhauled business documentation standards — improving reporting clarity, reducing data ambiguity, and building systematic record-keeping processes.
Projects04

Live Projects

From live AI-powered analytics platforms to professional Excel dashboards — each project built from real data, each downloadable or accessible online.

⬤ LiveAI-PoweredBI AnalyticsData ScienceMachine Learning2026
BI Copilot — AI Analytics Platform

A fully live, AI-powered Business Intelligence platform built and deployed to the web. Upload any CSV dataset and instantly get automated KPI dashboards, revenue analysis, profitability heatmaps, regional breakdowns, and ML-ready forecasting. Features 15+ pre-built analytical queries (Revenue by Region, Top 5 Products, Profit Margin Analysis, Anomaly Detection), interactive chart switching, and an AI Copilot layer. Demonstrates end-to-end skills from data ingestion to insight delivery — the applied outcome of completing the Google Advanced Data Analytics Certificate.

Case StudyExcelBusiness ManagementCost Analysis
Business Expense Pattern Case Study

Real-world expense analysis from managing family business finances. Bar chart analysis reveals Rent (₹8,000) and Shopping (₹4,200) together account for 57% of total monthly spend — a category concentration risk. The analysis informed concrete cost-control decisions, demonstrating direct financial management experience within a live business environment.
